cards.front: What is an Access Modifier for a method in Java?
cards.back: Used to specify access level to the method (public, private, protected, default)

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Constructor in Java?
cards.back: To initialize the objects of a class when they are created

cards.front: What is the value of the 'this' keyword in Java?
cards.back: Refers to the current instance of the class and is used to access variables and methods

cards.front: What is Garbage Collection in Java?
cards.back: A process by which the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) automatically frees up memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use

cards.front: When are objects eligible for Garbage Collection in Java?
cards.back: When they are no longer reachable by any reference variables or when their finalizers are not called
